task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

budget_vs_actuals_q2.xlsx
helios_architecture_overview.txt
helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt

meridian_contract_signed.pdf
office_lease_renewal.txt
project_helios_status.txt

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early
QA and testing,22000,20100,-1900,Under budget; QA resource joined 2 weeks late
Travel and client meetings,12000,9400,-2600,Two site visits deferred to Q3
Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

Project Helios — Team Roster

Name: Sarah Chen        | Title: Project Manager       | Email: s.chen@novabridge.com
Name: Derek Okafor      | Title: Technical Lead         | Email: d.okafor@novabridge.com
Name: Amir Hassan       | Title: Senior Backend Engineer| Email: a.hassan@novabridge.com
Name: Liu Wei           | Title: Backend Engineer       | Email: l.wei@novabridge.com
Name: Fatima Al-Rashid  | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: f.alrashid@novabridge.com
Name: Tomas Rivera      | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: t.rivera@novabridge.com
Name: Kenji Watanabe    | Title: QA Engineer            | Email: k.watanabe@novabridge.com
Name: Nadia Petrov      | Title: DevOps Engineer        | Email: n.petrov@novabridge.com
Name: Marcus Green      | Title: Data Engineer          | Email: m.green@novabridge.com

input.content:
Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios remains broadly ON TRACK, but schedule and delivery confidence are under pressure from several active risks. Core architecture and API gateway work are complete, and the team is progressing on data ingestion, dashboard development, and integration testing. However, Q2 carryover items, data-quality blockers, UAT access dependencies, performance concerns, and a pending GeoSync license renewal create a narrow path to the confirmed July 25 Meridian product demo and the October 1 target launch.

Leadership should treat the next two weeks as critical. The July 25 demo must use live Meridian data, and unresolved ingestion, performance, licensing, or environment issues could damage client confidence and increase downstream launch risk. Q3 financial exposure is also increasing, with a projected H2 overrun of approximately $47,000 and internal overruns not recoverable from Meridian under the MSA.

Overall Health
Status: ON TRACK, with material near-term risks
Schedule: AMBER — Q2 carryover and UAT dependency risks remain active
Scope: AMBER — informal feature requests are emerging; proposed Q3 additions require change control
Budget: AMBER/RED — Q2 actuals exceeded budget by $20,000; H2 projected overrun is $47,000
Quality: AMBER — sprint testing has open high-severity defects in ingestion and large-file handling
Client Confidence: AMBER — Meridian has confirmed expectations for a live-data demo on July 25

1. Current Progress

Completed or Stable Areas
- Requirements documentation v1.0: Complete
- System architecture design: Complete
- API gateway implementation: Complete and delivered
- Development environment setup: Complete
- Data schema finalization: Complete
- Authentication flow testing: 4/4 tests passed in Sprint 14

Current Delivery Progress
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22
- Integration testing: 40% complete; sign-off target 2026-07-25
- Sprint 14 testing: 10 of 15 planned test cases executed; 2 failed, 3 not yet run

Q2 Deliverables Still Carrying Into Q3
- Data ingestion module: In progress; target 2026-07-18
- Client dashboard v1: In progress; target 2026-07-22
- Integration test suite setup: Overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20
- UAT environment provisioning: Overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access, now expected by 2026-07-19
- Performance benchmarking report: Overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor

Sprint 14 Task Status Highlights
- HEL-244, integration test suite for auth flows: Done
- HEL-243, date format normalization: Blocked pending Meridian clean sample data due 2026-07-15
- HEL-246, UAT server provisioning: At risk
- HEL-247, dashboard load performance benchmark: Not started; due 2026-07-18
- HEL-248, GeoSync license renewal coordination: In progress; due 2026-07-14

2. Key Risks

Highest-Priority Active Risks

R-01: Meridian source data quality issues
- Likelihood: High; Impact: High; Risk score: 9
- Current issue: Meridian source data uses inconsistent date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
- Testing impact: Date normalization for MM/DD/YYYY is failing and affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data.
- Status: Blocked pending Meridian clean sample data, due 2026-07-15.
- Owner: Amir Hassan
- Leadership concern: If sample data is late or incomplete, ingestion completion and the July 25 live-data demo are at risk.

R-02: GeoSync license not renewed before demo
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6
- Status: Legal review in progress; target resolution 2026-07-14.
- Owner: Raj Patel / action item assigned to Derek Okafor in client sync
- Leadership concern: If the license is unresolved, mapping functionality or demo readiness could be affected. Potential additional cost: $12,000 if not renewed before expiry.

R-03: Dashboard performance on large datasets
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High; Risk score: 6
- Status: Benchmarking task HEL-247 has not started; due 2026-07-18.
- Client concern: Meridian explicitly raised dashboard load-time concerns.
- Q3 target: Dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Leadership concern: Performance uncertainty could affect the July 25 demo and later UAT acceptance.

R-04: UAT timeline slippage
- Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 6
- Status: One-week extension already consumed; UAT now scheduled for August 17-28.
- Leadership concern: Slippage greater than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

R-06: Meridian IT environment access delays
- Likelihood: Medium; Impact: Medium; Risk score: 4
- Current dependency: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT access expected by 2026-07-19.
- Owner: Nadia Petrov
- Leadership concern: Environment delays directly affect UAT readiness and production preparation.

Additional Risks to Monitor
- HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion over 200k records causes memory overflow; pipeline crashes around 210k records. Severity: High. This may threaten the Q3 ingestion throughput target of 500k records/min sustained.
- Key person dependency: Derek Okafor is technical lead and owner of performance benchmarking; no backup tech lead identified.
- Scope creep: Three informal Meridian feature requests logged; proposed Q3 additions include scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting, and a self-service mapping rule editor. These should not proceed without formal change control.
- Budget pressure: Q2 was $20,000 over budget, and projected H2 overrun is $47,000. Internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group under MSA Section 4.2.

3. Upcoming Q3 Commitments

July Commitments
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to provide IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
- 2026-07-14: Resolve GeoSync license renewal
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to unblock date-format issue
- 2026-07-18: Complete data ingestion module
- 2026-07-18: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian
- 2026-07-18: Complete dashboard load performance benchmark
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access
- 2026-07-20: Integration test suite setup rescheduled completion date
- 2026-07-20: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO
- 2026-07-22: Complete client dashboard v1
- 2026-07-25: Integration test sign-off target
- 2026-07-25: Product demo for Meridian stakeholders
  - Must include live data ingestion using Meridian sample data
  - Must show end-to-end pipeline walkthrough
  - Must show dashboard visualization with real data per contract Section 9.3

August Commitments
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian UAT window
- Deploy to UAT environment
- Support Meridian user testing sessions
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted
- Begin/continue performance hardening activities

August–September Commitments
- Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records
- Ingestion throughput benchmark target: 500k records/min sustained
- External security penetration test; vendor still TBD

September Commitments
- Production environment setup
- Runbook documentation
- Hypercare plan finalization using 30-day post-launch support model
- Final client training sessions

Target Launch
- Target production launch date: 2026-10-01
- Key constraint: UAT slippage greater than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger penalty review under contract Section 7.1.

4. Issues Requiring Leadership Attention

A. Protect the July 25 Demo
Leadership should ensure cross-functional escalation is available through the demo date. The demo is a client-visible milestone with a live-data requirement. Failure modes include late Meridian sample data, unresolved GeoSync licensing, unresolved ingestion defects, dashboard performance problems, or incomplete dashboard functionality.

Recommended leadership action:
- Establish a daily executive-visible demo readiness checkpoint from 2026-07-14 through 2026-07-25.
- Require clear go/no-go criteria by 2026-07-22.
- Confirm contingency plan if Meridian sample data arrives late or is unusable.

B. Escalate Meridian Dependencies
Meridian has dependencies due on 2026-07-12 and 2026-07-15 that are critical to environment setup and data defect resolution.

Recommended leadership action:
- Ask the client sponsor, James Whitfield, to reinforce timely delivery of the IT infrastructure diagram and clean sample dataset.
- If either item misses its date, escalate immediately through the client governance channel.

C. Resolve GeoSync License Before 2026-07-14
The license renewal is a near-term legal/procurement dependency with demo impact and possible incremental cost.

Recommended leadership action:
- Request Legal/Procurement prioritization and same-day escalation if renewal terms are blocked.
- Confirm whether the $12,000 exposure is already included in the Q3 forecast and whether contingency reserve can be applied.

D. Address Performance and Large-File Ingestion Risk
Two performance-related concerns are active: dashboard load time has not yet been benchmarked, and large-file ingestion fails around 210k records.

Recommended leadership action:
- Assign additional engineering support to Amir Hassan and Derek Okafor if root cause is not identified by 2026-07-15.
- Require preliminary performance benchmark results before the demo agenda is finalized.
- Consider naming a backup technical lead to reduce dependency on Derek Okafor.

E. Tighten Budget Governance
Q2 actuals were $322,000 against a $302,000 budget, a $20,000 overrun. Additional H2 overrun exposure is projected at $47,000, including GeoSync renewal risk and the UAT extension.

Recommended leadership action:
- Review the budget reconciliation plan before the 2026-07-20 CFO deadline.
- Decide whether to use contingency reserve, reduce non-critical scope, or seek formal change orders for any client-driven additions.
- Do not allow proposed Q3 feature additions to proceed without change control approval.

F. Preserve Launch Date Through UAT Discipline
The UAT window has already been extended by one week to August 17-28. Further slippage threatens the October 1 launch and could trigger penalty review.

Recommended leadership action:
- Lock UAT entry criteria by late July.
- Confirm UAT environment readiness no later than 2026-07-19.
- Require weekly leadership review of UAT readiness and defect aging starting immediately after the July 25 demo.

5. Recommended Near-Term Decisions

1. Approve a leadership-sponsored demo readiness war room through July 25.
2. Escalate Meridian dependencies through James Whitfield if the infrastructure diagram or clean sample dataset are late.
3. Direct Legal/Procurement to close GeoSync renewal by July 14 or present an alternative plan.
4. Assign backup technical coverage for Derek Okafor and additional support for ingestion/performance defects if needed.
5. Enforce formal change control for all proposed Q3 additions and informal Meridian feature requests.
6. Review and approve a budget reconciliation plan before the CFO review cycle.

Bottom Line
Project Helios can still meet the July 25 demo, August UAT, and October 1 launch targets, but the margin for delay is narrowing. Leadership attention is needed now on client dependencies, licensing, performance readiness, budget governance, and scope control.

content.text: Done — I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
